page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g6a.16xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance based on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 7H12 processor operating at a base frequency of 2.6 GHz. The instance provides 64 dedicated physical cores with a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration, yielding 64 vCPUs. This dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing capabilities without resource sharing.

The instance is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, maintaining a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage, requiring external block storage volumes for persistent data. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 16 Gbps. No graphics processing units are included in this configuration.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.g6a.16xlarge offers a balanced resource density suitable for enterprise workloads that require dedicated CPU execution without the premium cost of memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware. The 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it appropriate for multi-threaded applications, web serving, application servers, and general-purpose enterprise databases.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g6a.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7H12 processor running at 2.6 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It features 64 dedicated physical cores with a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration, providing 64 vCPUs and eliminating resource contention. The system is configured with 256.0 GB of RAM, establishing a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio. It does not include local storage or GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 16 Gbps. This instance is designed for workloads requiring consistent CPU performance and a balanced memory profile, such as enterprise application servers, web serving, and medium-sized database deployments.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g6a.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 64 dedicated AMD EPYC 7H12 vCPUs, 256 GB of RAM, and 16 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on a KVM hypervisor.

tagline: A dedicated sixty-four core AMD EPYC instance featuring a balanced memory-to-CPU ratio and sixteen gigabits per second network bandwidth.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated physical AMD EPYC 7H12 cores with 1.0 thread per core
256.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio
16 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for consistent data transfer
KVM hypervisor virtualization on x86_64 architecture
Zero local storage and no integrated GPU accelerators
Suitable for enterprise application servers and general-purpose databases
